title: gagea arvensis subsp. granatellii
intro: Gagea arvensis subsp. granatellii, also known as the Woodlark's-colt's-foot or the Spring snowflake, is a plant species within the genus Gagea. It is a bulbous perennial that is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers. Gagea arvensis subsp. granatellii has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is used to treat various health issues such as bronchitis, fever, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which have been used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
